string.text: {RETINUE} looted and equipped {ITEM} from your casualties.
And {COUNT} more pieces of gear were taken.
All parties can recruit custom troops (recommended for compatibility with other recruitment mods)
Only faction parties can recruit custom troops
Only the player can recruit custom troops
At least one battle equipment set must remain enabled for field battles
At least one battle equipment set must remain enabled for naval battles
At least one battle equipment set must remain enabled for this battle type
At least one battle equipment set must remain enabled for siege battles
Civilian equipment sets do not have battle type restrictions
Captains share the formation class of their base troops
You need {COST} denars to buy {ITEM}, but you only have {GOLD}.
Cannott remove a unit that still has upgrades
Clan troops are available from the start
Unlocks when the clan acquires its first fief
Not available in the Universal Editor
How should the new unit's equipment be initialized?
This clan has no custom troops.
The player clan's troops cannot be deleted.
This doctrine has already been acquired
Allies contribute to equipment unlock progress.
Hero kills count twice toward unlocking enemy gear.
Gear can be unlocked from allied casualties.
You need {COST} denars to unlock this doctrine.
You need {COST} influence to unlock this doctrine.
Increases skill caps by +5 for all troops.
Grants +20 skill points to elite troops.
Grants +20 skill points to basic troops.
Unlocks custom villager troops for village parties.
Unlocks Captain variants for all regular troops.
Unlocks custom militia troops for your faction's towns and castles.
You must be in an owned faction fief (retinues excepted)
You must be in any settlement
You can only edit this unit in a fief owned by {FACTION}.
You can only edit units while inside a settlement.
Cannot determine this unit's faction for editing restrictions.
Enter a surname or title (leave blank to remove):
Cannot remove the last equipment set
Cannot remove the last equipment set enabled for a battle type.
Heroes cannot have multiple equipment sets
Copying equipment from {SRC} to {DST} requires {COST} denars, but you only have {GOLD}.
Nothing new from {SRC} can be equipped on {DST}.
Hero data is tied to the save state.
The file could not be written.
Choose a file name for this export:
A file named '{NAME}' already exists.{newline}{newline}Do you want to overwrite it?
{newline}{newline}Use the banner icons at the top of the Troops screen to select which troop trees to edit.
{COUNT} new troops are now available for the {FACTION} {FACTION_TYPE}.
Field a unit wearing equipment worth over 100,000 denars.
Field a unit wearing equipment weighing over 60 kg.
Win a tournament in a town of your clan's culture.
Win a battle fielding only troops wearing full metal armor.
Defeat a ruler of a different culture in battle.
Get {TARGET} kills in battle with troops wearing no foreign gear.
Get {TARGET} kills with custom kingdom troops.
Have a companion of the same culture as your kingdom govern a kingdom fief for {TARGET} days.
Outnumbered at least 2 to 1, win a battle while fielding only retinues.
Win a battle against overwhelming odds while fielding mostly retinues.
Have a retinue get the first melee kill in a siege assault.
Have your retinues defeat {TARGET} enemy troops of equivalent tier without a single casualty.
Maintain a retinue-only party's morale above 90 for {TARGET} days.
Win a defensive battle with a retinue-only party.
Win by yourself against 100 or more enemies without a single death on your side.
Save a caravan or villager party from an enemy attack.
Win a battle of 100 or more combatants using only your retinues.
Have {TARGET} retinues survive being struck down in battle.
Complete a quest for an allied lord.
Complete a quest for a lord of your clan's culture.
Personally defeat {TARGET} enemies in one battle.
Win a battle in which allies suffer over 100 casualties.
Single-handedly win a battle against an enemy army of a different culture.
Personally defeat an enemy lord in battle.
Personally defeat 5 tier 5+ troops in one battle.
Capture a fief of your own culture from an enemy kingdom.
Win a battle while part of an allied lord's army.
Rescue a captive lord from an enemy party.
Win a battle where you are not the main commander.
Turn the tide of a battle involving an allied army.
Get {TARGET} kills with elite faction troops.
Knock out {TARGET} opponents in the arena.
Win a battle against over 100 enemies using a party evenly split among infantry, cavalry and ranged clan troops.
Defeat a party twice your size using only faction troops.
Upgrade {TARGET} elite faction troops to the next tier.
Upgrade {TARGET} faction troops to the next tier.
Lead an army for {TARGET} days in a row.
Win a siege defense fielding only faction troops.
In a single battle, get a kill using five different weapon classes.
Max out the skills of {TARGET} faction troops.
Raise the security value of a fief to {TARGET}.
Win {TARGET} defensive battles in a row.
Defend a city against a besieging army.
Complete a quest for a village headman.
Complete a quest for a village landowner.
Complete a quest for a town merchant notable.
Defend a village against an enemy raid.
Personally slay {TARGET} assailants during a siege defense.
Own {TARGET} caravans at the same time.
Max out the skills of a T5 basic troop.
Max out the skills of a T6 elite troop.
Raise the militia value of a fief to {TARGET}.
Type to filter the list by name, culture or tier.
Type to filter the list by name, category or tier.
Let the game automatically choose the formation class based on equipment
Choose how this unit is categorized. Leave on 'Auto' to categorize it based on its equipment.
Immortals: {REVIVED} of {TOTAL} fallen {TROOP} survived grievous wounds.
How do you want to create the troops for {CLAN}?
Retinues mod dependencies failed to initialize properly:{newline}{newline}{DETAILS}{newline}{newline}The mod may not function correctly until the dependencies are fixed.
Do you want to create a new equipment set by copying the current set or create an empty one?
Are you sure you want to delete the current equipment set? This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete {UNIT_NAME}? This action cannot be undone.
This name contains no valid file characters.
Unlocks when the player founds a kingdom
This will permanently delete the export file.{newline}{newline}Continue?
The file could not be deleted.
Permanently deletes this library item and associated XML file
This will open the export XML in your default editor.{newline}{newline}Continue?
The file could not be opened.
Directly edit this export's XML file contents

This will generate a standalone module.{newline}{newline}- Module id: {ID}{newline}- Target folder: {PATH}{newline}- Characters: {COUNT}{LIST}{newline}{newline}If the game install folder is protected, the module will be written here instead:{newline}{FALLBACK}{OVERWRITE}{newline}{newline}Continue?
Only vanilla troop edits can be converted to standalone mods
The file could not be exported.
Could not locate the game's Modules folder.
No characters could be generated from this export.
Nothing could be exported from this file.
This export type cannot be converted
Convert this export into a standalone mod
Troops and factions can be exported from the editor tab.
The file could not be imported.
The export could not be imported.
No faction is selected in the editor.
No factions are available to be overridden in the current editor mode.
Could not resolve the player clan.
No troops are available to be replaced in the current faction.
Select the faction to override with this import.
Select the troop to replace in {FACTION}.

Three optional systems add further constraints to equipment changes: a staging delay before items take effect, weight and value budgets per tier, and an unlock gate on individual items.{newline}{newline}Staging: When Equipping Takes Time is enabled, equipping an item does not take effect immediately. Instead the new item is placed into a staging queue and applied gradually as in-game time passes. Staged slots are displayed in a different color and show the currently equipped item in the tooltip. You can unstage an item at any time. Progress accumulates hour by hour and also while travelling if Equip While Travelling is enabled. Hero troops are never subject to staging; their equipment changes are always instant.{newline}{newline}Weight and Value Limits: The Limit By Weight and Limit By Value settings impose a budget on the total weight and total value of a troop's battle equipment. Both are off by default. When active, each troop tier has a corresponding budget shown in the editor. The Armory doctrines can raise these limits when they are active.{newline}{newline}Item Availability and Unlocks: When Equipment Needs Unlocking is enabled, each item requires 1000 points of unlock progress before it can be equipped. Items begin locked and hidden; once they have some progress they appear at the bottom of the list, sorted by completion. A configurable number of items per slot are pre-unlocked automatically on a new game up to a configurable tier ceiling. This system only applies in the Troops screen; in the Universal Editor all items are freely available.

The equipment economy tracks how many copies of each item your forces own and optionally charges gold when you equip gear that is not already in stock.{newline}{newline}Shared Items: When a troop has multiple sets, items are shared intelligently between them. If a piece of equipment is already present in another set belonging to the same troop, equipping it in a second set does not incur an additional stock cost or purchase. A troop with two sets wearing the same sword in both only requires one copy of that sword.{newline}{newline}Stocks: Each item has a stock count representing how many copies your forces have stockpiled. When economy is active, equipping an item draws one copy from its stock; unequipping returns it. Stock accumulates from battles, discards, and other sources. This system only applies in the Troops screen; in the Universal Editor stocks play no role.{newline}{newline}Costs and Purchases: When Equipment Costs Money is active, equipping an item that is not in stock and not covered by another set requires spending coin at the item's value scaled by the Cost Multiplier setting. Two doctrines reduce costs: Cultural Pride grants a 20% discount on items matching your clan's culture, and Royal Patronage grants a 20% discount on items matching your kingdom's culture. Both discounts can apply to the same item.

Each troop has two categories of equipment: battle sets and civilian sets. Battle sets are worn in combat; civilian sets at all other times. You switch between them using the button above the character model.{newline}{newline}Battle Set Types: Each battle set can be marked as applying to field battles, siege battles, or naval battles. A set can cover any combination of those types. At least one set must remain enabled for each battle type at all times.{newline}{newline}Managing Sets: Use the add button to create a copy of the current set or an empty one. Use the remove button to delete the current set; you cannot delete a set if it is the only one in its category, or if doing so would leave any battle type without coverage. When economy is active and a set is deleted, its items are returned to stock automatically.{newline}{newline}Slots: Each set has four weapon slots, Head, Cape, Body, Gloves, Leg, Horse, and Horse Harness. The Horse Harness slot is only active when a horse is equipped. If a new horse is equipped and the current harness is not compatible, the harness is cleared automatically.

Retinues adds a suite of systems for building, training, and equipping your own personal forces throughout a campaign.{newline}{newline}At its core is the retinue: a household troop tied to your clan that follows you exclusively and can be developed into an elite unit over time.{newline}{newline}Beyond retinues, you can build full custom troop trees for your clan and kingdom. These troops appear as volunteers and fill your faction's armies. A doctrine system provides a progression layer, unlocking new troop types, equipment perks, and other bonuses as you accomplish gameplay objectives.{newline}{newline}All of this is managed through the editor, opened from the Troops button on the campaign map or by pressing [R].

Non-player clans can also have their own retinues. This feature is enabled by default and applies to clans that meet a minimum tier threshold (tier 3 by default). Each day there is a 25% chance by default that an eligible party promotes one matching-tier troop into a retinue, up to a cap of 10% of the party's size. Kingdom rulers get a King's Guard or Queen's Guard in addition to their clan retinues.{newline}{newline}By default only the clan leader's party builds retinues. The Only Clan Leaders Have Retinues setting can be turned off to allow all lord parties in a clan to have their own.{newline}{newline}AI retinues improve over time as their clan earns experience. If you lose a battle against a clan that has retinues, their retinues can acquire equipment from your casualties, gradually upgrading their gear.

This section covers the party size limits on retinues, how to recruit them through the party upgrade system, the passive buffs they receive, and how to rank them up.{newline}{newline}Party Limits: The number of retinues you can field is capped at a ratio of your party size limit. The default is 15%, adjustable in settings. Doctrine bonuses can raise this cap further. Retinues cannot be placed in garrisons or transferred to companion parties; they follow you exclusively.{newline}{newline}Recruitment: Retinues do not appear in settlement volunteer lists. Instead they surface as upgrade options on existing troops in your party. When you open the party screen, each retinue is matched to the best fitting troop at one tier below it, and the retinue appears as an additional upgrade target on that troop. Upgrading a troop into a retinue converts one unit of the source troop into the retinue type without consuming the retinue slot. The source troop is chosen from your faction's custom tree first, falling back to your culture's tree if no match is found.{newline}{newline}Buffs: Retinues receive a set of passive bonuses on top of their normal stats. By default these are +20 hit points, +5 to all skill caps, and +20 to total skill points. Each bonus can be toggled and adjusted individually in settings. The Realistic preset disables all three.{newline}{newline}Ranking Up: When a retinue has its skills fully maxed out, it becomes eligible to rank up. Ranking up costs gold: (current tier + 1) x 1000 denars. It also costs skill points: 5 x current tier. The effect raises the retinue's level by 5 and unlocks higher skill caps. The rank up action is shown in the editor once all conditions are met.

Retinues are special household guard troops tied to your clan. Unlike regular faction troops they are personal to the player: they cannot be garrisoned or transferred to another party, and they stay with you at all times. They start at a low tier but can be trained, equipped, and ranked up over the course of a campaign.{newline}{newline}Starting Retinue: One retinue is created automatically when you start a new campaign or when you load an existing save for the first time. It is named after your clan and matches your clan's culture. If you are already a ruler at that point, a King's Guard or Queen's Guard is also created for your kingdom.

You can unlock one additional retinue per culture. Your own clan's culture is unlocked from the start. For every other culture, you accumulate unlock progress through gameplay activities tied to that culture.{newline}{newline}Winning a tournament awards +50 points for the tournament settlement's culture. Completing a quest successfully awards +25 points for the quest giver's culture. Owning a fief awards +5 points per day per fief for each fief's culture. Owning a workshop awards +2 points per day per workshop for the workshop settlement's culture. Winning a battle alongside allied parties awards +10 points per allied party for each ally's culture.{newline}{newline}Each culture requires 1000 points to unlock. The overall speed can be adjusted with the Retinue Unlock Speed setting. When a culture is unlocked, a new retinue is created automatically and a popup appears to let you jump directly to the editor.

This section covers the optional shared skill point pool, how spending and refunds work, and the per-tier caps and totals that bound the total skills of any troop.{newline}{newline}Shared Skill Points Pool: When the Shared Skill Points Pool setting is enabled, all custom troops contribute their battle XP to a single campaign-wide pool instead of per-troop pools. Skill points from the pool can be spent on any custom troop, making it easier to develop specialists without spreading battles evenly across your roster. The pool balance is shown in the skill panel header. Switching off the setting mid-campaign does not clear either the per-troop points or the shared pool. The setting requires Skill Points Must Be Earned to be active.{newline}{newline}Spending: Each skill increase costs one skill point; decreasing a skill refunds it immediately. When Training Takes Time is enabled, decreasing a skill that is already committed prompts a confirmation.{newline}{newline}Skill Caps and Totals: Two limits apply in the Troops screen. The skill cap is the maximum level any single skill can reach for that tier; defaults range from 20 at tier 0 to 360 at tier 7. The skill total is the sum of all skills a troop of that tier may have; defaults range from 90 at tier 0 to 1600 at tier 7. Retinues receive bonuses on top of both limits. Max-tier Captains receive a 20% bonus. The Iron Discipline doctrine adds +5 to the cap for all troops. Steadfast Soldiers and Masters at Arms each add +20 to the total for the basic and elite lines respectively.

Your custom troops have a skill sheet you can edit from the Troops screen, subject to per-skill caps and a total skill budget. This system does not apply to troops edited through the Universal Editor, where skills can be freely adjusted at any time.{newline}{newline}XP Sources: Custom troops accumulate skill point XP from played battles, autoresolved battles, daily party training, and other activities. When Skill Points Must Be Earned is active (on by default), all of that XP feeds into the skill point progress counter for the troop type. Higher-tier troops require proportionally more XP. The overall rate is adjustable with the Skill Points Gain Rate setting.{newline}{newline}Freeform Mode: When Skill Points Must Be Earned is disabled, skill points are not gated behind battle performance. Skills can be freely allocated in the editor at any time without earning anything first.

Three settings add further structure to skill editing: optional time-gating on training, a floor derived from parent troops, and location-based restrictions on when the editor can be used.{newline}{newline}Training Takes Time: When this setting is enabled, raising a skill in the editor does not apply instantly. The increase is stored as pending and applied gradually at the rate set by Trained Points Per Day (default: 1 per day). Pending increases are shown in a different color. Training progress continues while travelling by default; the Train While Travelling setting can restrict it to when the party is stationary.{newline}{newline}Skill Floor: A troop's skills cannot be lowered below the highest value held by any of its upgrade sources. For example, if a tier-2 source troop has 80 in One Handed, the tier-3 upgrade's One Handed cannot be set below 80. The editor shows which source troop is setting the floor when a decrease is blocked.{newline}{newline}Context Restrictions: The Editing Restriction setting can limit when skills and equipment can be changed. None (default in Freeform) places no restrictions. In Settlement requires you to be inside any settlement. In Fief requires you to be inside a settlement owned by your clan or kingdom. Retinues are exempt from the fief requirement and can be edited in any settlement.

When a custom troop tree is first created, two settings determine the shape of its starting roster, and several more control where those troops appear as volunteers.{newline}{newline}Starter Troops: The initial state of the tree depends on the Starter Troops setting. Roots Only creates only the two root troops, leaving the rest empty to build manually. Lean Trees (default) copies a streamlined version of your culture's tree: one representative troop per formation type per tier, with faction-based names like Clan Recruit. Full Trees clones the entire culture tree verbatim, replacing the culture prefix with your clan or kingdom name.{newline}{newline}Starter Equipment: Equipment is set separately by the Starter Equipment setting. Random Set (default) generates a random loadout from items appropriate for each troop's tier and culture. Single Set copies the first equipment set from the culture template. All Sets copies every equipment set from the template. Empty Set creates troops with no items at all.{newline}{newline}Recruitment: Where your custom troops appear as volunteers depends on the Clan Troops Availability and Kingdom Troops Availability settings. Clan troops default to clan fiefs and kingdom troops to kingdom fiefs. Both can be opened to any settlement or restricted further. The Same Culture Only setting prevents custom troops from appearing in settlements of a different culture. The Allowed Recruiters setting controls whether AI lords can also recruit your troops. By default custom troops replace standard culture volunteers entirely in eligible settlements; Mix With Vanilla Troops lets them appear alongside standard troops instead.

Beyond your retinues, you can build and customize full troop trees for your clan and your kingdom. These troops appear in volunteer pools, garrisons, and parties across the world and are edited from the same Troops screen as retinues. Each faction gets two root lines: a basic tree and an elite noble tree. From those roots you can branch upgrade paths up to a maximum of 4 upgrade targets per troop.{newline}{newline}Unlocking: Clan troops unlock when you acquire your first fief by default. They can also be set to unlock from the start or disabled entirely. Kingdom troops unlock when you found a kingdom by default, and can also be disabled. When troops are unlocked a popup appears to let you jump straight to the new tree.

Several doctrines unlock additional troop types beyond the main tree, and the editor provides tools for managing the tree structure and overseeing other clans in your kingdom.{newline}{newline}Militias and Special Troops: Three doctrines unlock additional custom troop types. Stalwart Militia unlocks custom militia troops that replace the default cultural militia in your faction's towns and castles on a daily cycle. Road Wardens unlocks custom caravan troops used by your faction's caravans. Armed Peasantry unlocks custom villager troops used by your faction's village parties.{newline}{newline}Captains: The Captains doctrine unlocks Captain variants for all regular troops. A Captain is a stronger version that spawns periodically in its place. The spawn rate is configurable through the Captain Spawn Rate setting (default: 1 per 15 regular troops). Captains cannot have upgrade targets of their own and cannot be ranked up. Their equipment is edited separately but they share equipment costs with their base troop.{newline}{newline}Upgrades and Removal: You can add an upgrade target to any non-hero custom troop that is not at max tier and has fewer than 4 upgrade targets. The new troop is created one tier above its parent and inherits skills from it. To remove a troop you must first remove all of its upgrade targets. Root troops cannot be removed. When a troop is removed, any units of that type in the world are converted to their culture counterpart.{newline}{newline}Other Clans: When you are a ruler, the editor lets you manage the custom troop trees of other clans in your kingdom. Click the left banner to select a different clan. If the selected clan has no custom troops yet, you are prompted to initialize them by copying the kingdom's existing tree or starting from culture roots.
Captains share the mariner ability of their base troops
Mariner: better suited for naval combat, but earn skill points at a reduced rate
Mariner: better suited for naval combat
Maximum percentage of a lord party's size that retinue troops can occupy (1-50).
Chance per day that an eligible lord party converts one matching-tier troop into a retinue (1-100).
If enabled, only the clan leader's party will gain and use retinues. If disabled, all lord parties in the clan are eligible.
Minimum clan tier (1-6) required for a non-player clan to have their own retinues.
Determines who can recruit custom troops.
If enabled, retinue troops will receive a health bonus.
If enabled, retinue troops will receive a skill cap bonus.
If enabled, retinue troops will receive a skill total bonus.
Determines how many regular troops must be fielded before another captain can spawn.
Determines where clan troops can be recruited from.
Determines when clan troops become available.
The number of items unlocked per equipment slot on game start.
The maximum tier of items unlocked on game start.
Multiplier affecting the influence cost of acquiring doctrines.
Multiplier affecting the money cost of acquiring doctrines.
If enabled, acquiring doctrines will cost influence.
If enabled, acquiring doctrines will cost money.
Restricts where faction troops can be edited.
Enables the [R] hotkey to open the editor.
If enabled, non-player clans meeting the minimum tier will have their own retinues.
Toggles the Doctrines feature on or off.
If enabled, equipping troops will cost money. Also enables the stocks system.
If enabled, doctrines require specific feats to be accomplished before they can be acquired.
Toggles the Retinues feature on or off.
Enables the Universal Editor, that can be used to edit vanilla troops and heroes.
If enabled, the Universal Editor will enforce normal equipment limits when editing vanilla troops.
If enabled, the Universal Editor will enforce normal skill limits when editing vanilla troops.
Determines the cost to equip a new piece of equipment.
If enabled, troops will need to unlock equipment items before being able to equip them.
Whether to limit the total equipment value (limits are tier-based).
Affects the maximum total equipment value limit.
Affects the maximum total equipment weight limit.
Whether equipping progresses while the party is travelling or only while waiting in settlements.
If enabled, equipping troops will not be instant and will take time to be completed.
Determines how feat completion notifications are shown.
Determines how item unlock notifications are shown.
Determines where kingdom troops can be recruited from.
Determines when kingdom troops become available.
The maximum number of retinues as a ratio of the party size limit.
Troops below the specified tier will not be allowed to be mounted.
Troops below the specified tier will not be allowed to use noble mounts.
Troops below the specified tier will not be allowed to use war mounts.
If enabled, custom troops will be mixed with vanilla troops in recruitment pools.
Sets the chance for mixed gender units to spawn as a member of the opposite sex.
The maximum tier for randomly assigned starter equipment. Item tier is also capped by troop tier.
The number of days it takes one workshop to unlock an item.
The number of times an item must be discarded to unlock it.
The number of enemy troops wearing an item that must be defeated to unlock it.
The amount added to retinue troop health when Buff Retinue Health is enabled.
The amount added to retinue troop skill caps when Buff Retinue Skill Cap is enabled.
The amount added to retinue troop skill totals when Buff Retinue Skill Total is enabled.
Multiplier affecting how quickly new retinues are unlocked.
If enabled, custom troops can only be recruited in settlements of the same culture.
If enabled, all custom troops draw skill points from a single shared pool instead of each troop having its own.
Rate at which troops gain skill points in battle.
If enabled, troops will need to earn skill points in battle before leveling up skills.
Determines training speed in points per day.
Sets the starter equipment for newly unlocked troops.
Determines clan and kingdom starter troops.
Determines how long it takes to equip a new piece of equipment.
Whether training progress occurs while the party is travelling or only while waiting in settlements.
If enabled, increasing skills will not be instant and will take time to be improved.
Whether items are unlocked by discarding items.
Whether items are unlocked by defeating enemies in battles.
Whether owning workshops unlocks items over time. Items are selected based on workshop type and settlement culture.
Whether to limit the total equipment weight (limits are tier-based).
Enable doctrines and configure acquisition costs.
How equipment items are unlocked over time.
Where and who can recruit custom troops.
Where editing is allowed and other limits.
Core retinues feature, unlocking, and bonuses.
Maximum skill level per troop tier.
Maximum total skill points per troop tier.
Skill gain rules and training behavior.
Troop availability and starter generation options.
Settings for editing vanilla troops and heroes.
Allow unit to spawn in either gender
Disallow unit to spawn in either gender
Mixed Gender: {PERCENT}% will spawn as the opposite gender
No clans are loaded in the current game.
No cultures found in the current game.
No cultures are loaded in the current game.
This combination of gender, culture and species cannot be rendered.{newline}{newline}The previous appearance has been restored.
Writes a message to the in-game message log
Apply the Default preset: a balanced experience designed for a first playthrough

The default configuration, balanced for a first playthrough.{newline}{newline}Retinue buffs are active, clan troops unlock with your first fief, equipment has a cost, and items must be unlocked through kills and workshops before they can be equipped. Skill points must be earned in battle.

A creative sandbox: no costs, no requirements, no restrictions.{newline}{newline}Doctrine acquisition is free. Equipment is free and all items are immediately available. Skill points can be assigned freely with no experience requirement. Clan troops are available from the start and can be recruited anywhere.

A stricter experience that emphasises earned progression and faction identity.{newline}{newline}Skill and equipment limits are enforced in the Universal Editor. Troops can only be modified while in an owned fief. Retinue buffs are disabled. Equipping and training take time. Equipment is subject to tier-based weight and value limits. Troops can only be recruited in same-culture settlements, and only culture roots are generated at game start.
Apply the Freeform preset: removes costs, requirements, and restrictions
Apply the Realistic preset: enables time mechanics, location limits, and faction filters

Please select the settings that best match your playstyle.{newline}{newline}DEFAULT: A balanced first-playthrough experience. A full tree of clan troops are unlocked with your first fief, equipment has a cost, and skill points must be bought with experience earned in battle.{newline}{newline}FREEFORM: An unrestricted sandbox editor. No costs or unlock systems. Custom clan troops are available right from the start and can be recruited anywhere.{newline}{newline}REALISTIC: A grounded experience with harsher constraints. Troop editing is restricted to owned fiefs, equipments are limited in weight and value, equipping items and training skills take time, and troop trees must be built from scratch.{newline}{newline}You can fine-tune any individual setting at any time from the Settings tab in the Troops screen.
Purge all custom troop data from the game

This action is meant to help you uninstall Retinues safely.{newline}{newline}It will scan every party roster in the current campaign and remove or replace ALL custom troops created by Retinues.{newline}{newline}This action is irreversible. Make a backup save first. Continue?
Something went wrong while purging. Your save may be partially modified. Do not save, reload your game and try again, or report the issue.
Do you want to simply remove custom troops from rosters, or try to replace them with their closest vanilla equivalents when possible?
No running campaign. Load a save first.
Purge complete. Save the game into a new file and you will be able to remove Retinues safely from your game.
Purge finished but some troops could not be replaced ({MISSES}) and were removed instead.{newline}{newline}Consider running again in Remove mode, or report this issue.
Cannot change species for this unit
This species is not compatible with the current culture/gender.
No valid model exists for this species.
Rank up {NAME}?{newline}{newline}Costs:{newline}- {SP_COST} skill points{newline}- {GOLD_COST} gold{newline}{newline}Effect:{newline}- +5 levels (tier up)
Not available in the Universal Editor
Skills must be maxed out first
Reset {NAME} to its default values?{newline}{newline}This will undo all edits for this character.{newline}{newline}Save and restart the game for this action to take effect.
Reset which of the following to default values?
Only available in the Universal Editor
Save and restart the game for this action to take effect.
Reset all troops in {FACTION} to their default values?{newline}{newline}This will undo all edits for {COUNT} troop(s).{newline}{newline}Save and restart the game for this action to take effect.
No modified vanilla troops found to reset.
Only modified, non-hero vanilla troops can be reset.
You can now hire your newly acquired {RETINUE}.
{SOURCE}: +{DELTA}% progress towards unlocking {CULTURE} retinue. Total progress: {TOTAL}%.
Upgrade to {RETINUE}{newline}Max retinue cap reached: {CAP}{newline}Increase your party size limit to allow for more retinues
